MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1500
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF SYDNEY DAY's Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1500.

No appearances in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion and request for sanctions are granted as unopposed. The requests for admission attached as Exhibit A to the Declaration of Anthony Giles are deemed admitted. Defendant Ashbury General Contracting and Engineering is sanctioned in the amount of $1,500.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerks: M. Macadangdang, V. Da Fonseca; not reported. (525/JPT)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1500
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Continued To Nov-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2024 Continued To Feb-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1500
Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Anthony D. Giles, Esq., Law Office of Anthony Giles (415) 839-2099 anthony@anthonygiles.com
Attorney for Plaintiff
No other appeareances

Plaintiff's counsel sitpulates to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn deciding today's motion.

Hearing is held. Discussed not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SYDNEY DAY Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1500

The unopposed motion is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff's Notice of Motion is defective as it does not identify the party to whom Plaintiff's first set of Requests for Admission was served: "Plaintiff shall and hereby does move for an order deeming admitted Plaintiff's first set of Requests for Admission, and also for an order of monetary sanctions against the party Ashbury General Contracting and Engineering only." Consequently, there is NO notice of motion to deem requests for admission to any party. The way this notice is written, Plaintiff is seeking to have Plaintiff's first set of Requests for Admission to an unknown party deem admitted. Plaintiff is also seeking monetary sanctions against Defendant Ashbury General Contracting and Engineering only, presumably as opposed to seeking monetary sanctions against both party and counsel for the party. The improper use of appropriate punctuations renders this notice defective.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
